run.note: MEASURE (post-run, report-only): the prompt's REVIEW SURFACE law requires w6-verification ITSELF to run `npm run lesson:bbox` before judging any collision/size question. out/<id>/bbox-overlay/*.png is a SHARED side-effect path w4a-composer's own self-check also writes, so bare existence false-passes on a stale upstream leftover — this checks that at least one PNG's mtime is AT/AFTER w6-verification's own .pi/run.json startedAt (fail-closed if that timing is unavailable), i.e. THIS node actually ran the command, not merely inherited w4a's.

MEASURE (post-run, report-only): every cue script-cues.json declares must get an independently-addressed verdict row INSIDE a markdown table whose header names a "cue" column — a bare `| N |` anywhere in the document (a TOC, a frame-index list) does not count, only a row within a genuine per-cue table does. Catches both a silently-dropped cue and a coincidental-numbering false pass. Report-only; never blocks (see gen/check-cue-coverage.mjs).

MEASURE (post-run, report-only): every non-clean bbox-manifest.json summary signal (gatesFailed[]/measuredCollisionCount/captionIntrusionCount) must be ACKNOWLEDGED in verification.md — the lesson-verification SKILL forbids silent acceptance. An outright DENIAL of a measured signal ('no collisions found', 'overlap: clear') is factually false and FAILS same as an omission — mere keyword presence is not enough, the mention must not itself deny the signal. Mechanical completeness only; whether the justification is GOOD is criteria.md R2/R4's job.
